prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(initialize_assessment player_one)
(assign_loadout_marker player_one loadout_marker_a)
(attach_upgrade_module player_one upgrade_module_mk1)
(confirm_module_attachment player_one upgrade_module_mk1)
(bind_equipment_slot player_one equipment_slot_primary)
(reserve_and_prepare_node_a player_one node_a_1 upgrade_module_mk1)
(apply_equipment_slot_to_node_a player_one node_a_1 equipment_slot_primary)
(detach_upgrade_module player_one upgrade_module_mk1)
(initialize_assessment npc_one)
(assign_loadout_marker npc_one loadout_marker_b)
(attach_upgrade_module npc_one upgrade_module_mk1)
(confirm_module_attachment npc_one upgrade_module_mk1)
(consume_resource_for_node_b npc_one node_b_1 consumable_1)
(finalize_node_b_preparation npc_one node_b_1 upgrade_module_mk1 consumable_1)
(compose_and_stage_mission_asset player_one npc_one node_a_1 node_b_1 mission_asset_route_1)
(detach_upgrade_module npc_one upgrade_module_mk1)
(attach_upgrade_module player_one upgrade_module_mk1)
(validate_mission_asset mission_asset_route_1 player_one upgrade_module_mk1)
(commit_agent_rebuild player_one mission_asset_route_1)
(commit_npc_rebuild npc_one mission_asset_route_1)
(commit_rebuild_with_repair_material player_one repair_material_pack_1 upgrade_module_mk1)
(execute_rebuild_and_redeploy_player player_one loadout_marker_a repair_material_pack_1)
(detach_upgrade_module player_one upgrade_module_mk1)
(release_equipment_slot player_one equipment_slot_primary)
(attach_upgrade_module npc_one upgrade_module_mk1)
(commit_rebuild_with_repair_material npc_one repair_material_pack_1 upgrade_module_mk1)
(execute_rebuild_and_redeploy_npc npc_one loadout_marker_b repair_material_pack_1)
(detach_upgrade_module npc_one upgrade_module_mk1)
(initialize_assessment controller_alpha)
(assign_loadout_marker controller_alpha loadout_marker_c)
(attach_upgrade_module controller_alpha upgrade_module_mk1)
(confirm_module_attachment controller_alpha upgrade_module_mk1)
(initialize_upgrade_state controller_alpha upgrade_state_1 mission_asset_route_1)
(prime_controller_for_activation controller_alpha upgrade_state_1 mission_asset_route_1 upgrade_module_mk1)
(attach_special_attachment controller_alpha special_attachment_alpha)
(bind_equipment_slot controller_alpha equipment_slot_primary)
(engage_activation_sequence_variant_1 controller_alpha special_attachment_alpha equipment_slot_primary upgrade_state_1 mission_asset_route_1)
(attach_special_module controller_alpha special_module_alpha)
(activate_special_module_variant_1 controller_alpha special_module_alpha upgrade_state_1 mission_asset_route_1)
(persist_rebuild_commitment controller_alpha)
(commit_rebuild_with_repair_material controller_alpha repair_material_pack_1 upgrade_module_mk1)
(execute_rebuild_and_redeploy_controller controller_alpha loadout_marker_c repair_material_pack_1)
(detach_upgrade_module controller_alpha upgrade_module_mk1)
(release_equipment_slot controller_alpha equipment_slot_primary)
(detach_special_attachment controller_alpha special_attachment_alpha)
(detach_special_module controller_alpha special_module_alpha)
(initialize_assessment controller_bravo)
(assign_loadout_marker controller_bravo loadout_marker_c)
(attach_upgrade_module controller_bravo upgrade_module_mk1)
(confirm_module_attachment controller_bravo upgrade_module_mk1)
(initialize_upgrade_state controller_bravo upgrade_state_2 mission_asset_route_1)
(prime_controller_for_activation controller_bravo upgrade_state_2 mission_asset_route_1 upgrade_module_mk1)
(attach_special_attachment controller_bravo special_attachment_alpha)
(bind_equipment_slot controller_bravo equipment_slot_primary)
(engage_activation_sequence_variant_1 controller_bravo special_attachment_alpha equipment_slot_primary upgrade_state_2 mission_asset_route_1)
(attach_special_module controller_bravo special_module_alpha)
(activate_special_module_variant_1 controller_bravo special_module_alpha upgrade_state_2 mission_asset_route_1)
(persist_rebuild_commitment controller_bravo)
(commit_rebuild_with_repair_material controller_bravo repair_material_pack_1 upgrade_module_mk1)
(execute_rebuild_and_redeploy_controller controller_bravo loadout_marker_c repair_material_pack_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is a complex planning domain for rebuilding and redeploying agents: player_one (player_agent), npc_one (npc_agent), and two controllers: controller_alpha and controller_bravo.


## Examining agent roles  
- The problem involves a planning domain with agents: player_one (player_agent) and npc_one (npc_agent).  
- Two controllers, controller_alpha and controller_bravo, manage agent operations.  

## Defining the objective  
- The goal is to rebuild and redeploy the agents within the planning domain.  
- This requires coordinating actions between agents and controllers to achieve the desired state.

## Examining resource availability  
- Only one upgrade_module_mk1 is available, but four entities need configuration.  
- Resources include three loadout_markers (a, b, c), one equipment_slot_primary, consumable_1, and repair_material_pack_1.  

## Checking configuration steps  
- Entities must be assessed_for_redeployment and have loadout_assigned using available loadout_markers.  
- Configuration validation requires attaching upgrades and confirming, consuming the upgrade_module per deployable entity.  

## Analyzing mission asset setup  
- Composing the mission asset requires players and NPCs to be ready, with nodes primary ready and validated.  
- Players need upgrade_module_attached to be player_ready_for_asset, while NPCs follow similar readiness checks.  

## Exploring controller specifics  
- Controllers need controller_has_mission_asset, upgrade_state_available, and mission_asset_staged for initialization.  
- Controller_alpha and controller_bravo have different upgrade states (upgrade_state_1 and upgrade_state_2) and communication markers, with comm_marker_alpha available but comm_marker_bravo listed as held, not available.  

## Considering deployment constraints  
- Both controllers share mission_asset_route_1, but only one set may process at a time given limited resources.  
- Redeployment of all four entities (player_one, npc_one, controller_alpha, controller_bravo) is the goal, with potential primary/secondary outcome variants.

## Examining controller communication  
- Preparing controller communication markers requires config_validated, upgrade_attached, and comm_marker_available.  
- Comm_marker_alpha is available for controller_alpha, but comm_marker_bravo is held by controller_bravo and not initially available.  

## Analyzing resource constraints  
- Only one upgrade_module_mk1 is available, requiring sequential processing of entities for configuration.  
- Players and NPCs must be configured first to stage and validate the mission asset before handling controllers.  

## Exploring node preparation options  
- Reserve_and_prepare_node_a sets primary_ready when config_validated, upgrade_attached, and node_assigned.  
- Apply_equipment_slot_to_node_a then sets player_ready_for_asset and player_node_a_ready, avoiding consumable use.  

## Checking mission asset staging  
- Staging the mission asset requires player_ready_for_asset, npc_ready_for_asset, and nodes primary ready.  
- Combining reserve_and_prepare_node_a with apply_equipment_slot_to_node_a ensures necessary readiness conditions are met.

## Planning resource allocation  
- The system has only one upgrade_module_mk1, requiring sequential configuration of player and NPC to avoid conflicts.  
- Players can use the equipment path (no consumable needed), while NPCs can follow the consumable path, optimizing resource use.  

## Sequencing deployment steps  
- Configure the player first with upgrade and equipment, prepare their node, then detach for NPC configuration.  
- After player setup, use the consumable path for NPC, ensuring both achieve primary_ready and necessary readiness states for staging.  

## Validating mission staging  
- Compose and stage the mission asset after both player and NPC are ready, using mission_asset_route_1 and ensuring nodes are assigned.  
- Validation requires the upgrade attached to the player, highlighting the need for careful sequencing to manage resource availability.

## Assessing controller activation  
- Controller activation requires multiple steps, including priming, engaging, and activating special modules, with conditions like upgrade_attached and equipment_bound.  
- Activate_special_module variant1, suitable for plain asset staging, sets activation_finalized without strategy_attached, meeting persist_rebuild_commitment preconditions.  

## Evaluating resource limitations  
- Limited resources like special_attachment_alpha and special_module_alpha necessitate sharing, with options to detach and reattach for sequential controller processing.  
- Controllers may need to be redeployed last, requiring careful sequencing to manage equipment and special attachments effectively.  

## Planning controller readiness  
- Controller readiness for activation involves staging the mission asset without outcomes, ensuring validation and config_validated status.  
- Engage_activation variant1 needs has_special_attachment and equipment_bound, simplifying the process for controllers without secondary outcomes.

## Preparing player setup  
- Player one uses upgrade_module_mk1 for node_a_1, achieving primary_ready status.  
- Applying equipment_slot_primary to player ensures player_ready_for_asset and player_node_a_ready, keeping the upgrade attached for later validation.  

## Managing upgrade detachment  
- After player setup, detach upgrade_module_mk1 from player to make it available for NPC configuration.  
- This allows the upgrade to be temporarily reassigned to NPC for preparation, ensuring resource flexibility.  

## Configuring NPC consumable path  
- NPC configuration requires attaching upgrade_module_mk1 to achieve config_validated status, followed by consuming consumable_1 for node_b secondary_ready.  
- Finalizing NPC preparation with the upgrade ensures node_b primary_ready, setting both player and NPC ready for staging.  

## Staging and validating mission  
- Both player and NPC are ready; staging mission_asset_route_1 occurs after composing, ensuring all nodes are assigned and included.  
- Post-staging, detach the upgrade from NPC and reattach to player for validation, maintaining resource availability.

## Reviewing NPC and Player Sequences  
- NPC assessment and loadout occur after detaching the upgrade from the player, ensuring proper sequencing.  
- Upgrade is attached to NPC for finalize_node_b, then detached post-staging for reattachment to player for validation.  

## Ensuring Controller Readiness  
- Controllers use persist_rebuild_commitment without team or comm, leveraging shared special attachments and loadouts.  
- Execute_rebuild_and_redeploy_controller requires loadout_assigned, which is set by assign_loadout_marker, ensuring readiness.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
